report_name: Barracuda Warns of Rapid Qilin Ransomware and Spike in Brute-Force Attacks from Middle East

threat_actors.name: Qilin
threat_actors.description: A highly active Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) operation noted for its extreme speed of execution post-compromise. Analysis of a mitigated attack showed the malware can encrypt a network in minutes.

threat_actors.name: Middle East-based Threat Actors
threat_actors.description: Unspecified threat actors originating from the Middle East, responsible for a massive spike in brute-force authentication attacks against SonicWall and FortiGate devices in early 2026.

attack_patterns.name: Password Guessing
attack_patterns.description: Used in persistent, high-volume brute-force authentication attempts against network perimeter devices like SonicWall and FortiGate firewalls.

attack_patterns.name: Password Spraying
attack_patterns.description: Used as part of the high-volume brute-force authentication attempts against network perimeter devices to find valid credentials while avoiding account lockouts.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The Qilin ransomware payload, once executed, triggers widespread file encryption across the victim network almost instantly.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The goal of the brute-force attacks is to compromise valid accounts on perimeter devices to gain initial access to the corporate network.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: Ransomware like Qilin deletes volume shadow copies to prevent system recovery from local backups.

malware.name: Qilin Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware payload used by the Qilin group. It is noted for its rapid execution, capable of encrypting a network in minutes and deleting volume shadow copies to inhibit recovery.

tools.name: vssadmin
tools.description: Used by ransomware like Qilin to delete volume shadow copies, inhibiting system recovery.

identities.name: SonicWall
identities.description: The company whose firewall and VPN devices were targeted by a massive spike in brute-force authentication attacks.

identities.name: Fortinet
identities.description: The company whose FortiGate firewall and VPN devices were targeted by a massive spike in brute-force authentication attacks.

infrastructure.name: Middle East Brute-Force Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: A large set of IP addresses located in the Middle East used to conduct high-volume brute-force attacks against network perimeter devices.

indicators.name: High Volume of Failed Logins
indicators.pattern:
title: High Volume of Failed Logins
id: 2b1a3c4d-5e6f-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects a high volume of failed login attempts, which may indicate a brute-force attack as described in the Barracuda April 2026 report. This rule is intended for SIEMs that can correlate events; on its own it will fire on every failed login.
references:
  - https://blog.barracuda.com/2026/04/14/soc-threat-radar-april-2026/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-15
tags:
  - attack.t1110
  - attack.credential_access
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate users repeatedly failing to log in.
  - Misconfigured services.
level: low
logsource:
  product: windows
  service: security
detection:
  selection:
    EventID: 4625
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects failed logon attempts (Windows Event ID 4625). A high volume of these events from a single source or against a single target may indicate a brute-force attack.

indicators.name: Volume Shadow Copy Deletion via VSSAdmin
indicators.pattern:
title: Volume Shadow Copy Deletion via VSSAdmin
id: 8d7c6b5a-4f3e-4d2c-1b0a-987654321fed
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware TTP used to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://blog.barracuda.com/2026/04/14/soc-threat-radar-april-2026/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-15
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative activity, although rare.
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of 'vssadmin.exe delete shadows', a common technique used by ransomware like Qilin to inhibit system recovery.

indicators.name: Ransomware Canary File Creation
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Ransomware Canary File Creation
id: f0e9d8c7-b6a5-4f4e-3d2c-1b0a98765432
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of a known ransomware canary file 'canary.txt'. This is an indicator of potential ransomware activity.
references:
  - https://blog.barracuda.com/2026/04/14/soc-threat-radar-april-2026/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-15
tags:
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.impact
falsepositives:
  - Unlikely.
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\canary.txt'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of a 'canary.txt' file, a method for detecting ransomware file encryption activity.

relationships.source_name: Volume Shadow Copy Deletion via VSSAdmin
Volume Shadow Copy Deletion via VSSAdmin
